reviewers.error: model availability — tried gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini; all failed

reviewers.tooling_note: Dispatcher's review-manifest.json reported 'Timeout after 600s' but findings file was successfully written. The dispatcher's stdout/process-management timeout fired but gemini's file-write completed independently. Findings content is unambiguous evidence of successful round-2 completion (references trusted_hosts, observability/spec.md, Cross-Domain Redirect Rejection — all iteration-1 content). Filing as a parallel-infrastructure follow-up: dispatcher should check for findings-file existence after timeout before declaring failure.

convergence_summary: 2 vendors successful (claude_code + gemini); codex unavailable due to OpenAI capacity. Cross-vendor finding overlap minimal: gemini caught D3 return-type bug, Protocol lifecycle declaration gap, D10/D27 contradiction, and tmp-file race that claude self-review missed; claude caught https-only redirect, page-size-independent bound, and observability kwarg-consistency that gemini missed. Different blind spots — exactly the value-prop of cross-vendor convergence.

consensus_findings.description: trace_graph_call kwarg consistency: graph-client scenarios now assert retry_attempt and error kwargs
https-only scheme for @odata.nextLink redirects (TLS downgrade prevention)

consensus_findings.rejection_reason: msal Python's PublicClientApplication has internal thread-safe locking; concurrent acquire_token_silent calls within a single PCA instance are coalesced. The originally-flagged AAD-double-call concern does not occur. Note: gemini's GEM-P5-01 finding addresses a *different* aspect (multi-PCA-instance tmp-file race) which IS real and is fixed via C5 below.
consensus_findings.description: Concurrent token-cache refresh coalescing — superseded by gemini GEM-P5-01

consensus_findings.rejection_reason: msal-auth/spec.md L116-121 already specifies .cache/ directory creation with 0o700 if missing

consensus_findings.description: Token cache O_EXCL tmp-file race when same persona has multiple InteractiveDelegatedStrategy instances. Spec now requires distinct random-suffix tmp paths and stale-tmp cleanup, no fail-fast on collision.
design.md D3 get_bytes return type was '-> bytes' but spec mandates dict {path, size_bytes, content_type, request_id}. D3 now correctly declares dict[str, Any].
CloudGraphClient Protocol now declares __aenter__/__aexit__/aclose lifecycle methods. design.md D3 + graph-client spec Requirement 'CloudGraphClient Protocol' both updated.
bytes_streamed kwarg added to ObservabilityProvider.trace_graph_call signature in observability/spec.md
retry_attempt 0-indexed across observability AND graph-client specs (graph-client 401-then-success scenario already updated in claude_code C1 fix)
MSAL_FALLBACK_DEVICE_CODE scope clarified as process-level only; per-persona override deferred to P5b. New scenario added to msal-auth spec.
design.md D10 vs D27 MemoryPolicy contradiction resolved: D10 now consistently states minimal consumption per D27. Task 6.5 updated to assert minimal-prepend behavior instead of 'NOT consumed'.
wp-foundation 54-task decomposition. Architectural choice; awaiting human decision.
Pagination Discipline scenario now uses page-count-based bound, not page_size-dependent
trusted_hosts now documented in design.md D4 with default behavior
GraphAPIError parent-class __str__ URL sanitization scenario added (UPN local-part redaction)
Task 1.10 reference updated from 'Page ceiling triggers termination' to 'Page ceiling raises rather than terminates silently'

convergence_state.blocking_reason: C12 (wp-foundation decomposition) is escalated to human; not auto-fixable. All other 15 findings either fixed (12) or rejected as already-addressed/over-cautious (3).
convergence_state.next_action: Await human decision on C12. If accepted (defer to P5b or just keep 54-task foundation), declare convergence and proceed to IMPLEMENT. If wp-foundation should split now, run iteration 3 to apply the decomposition.
